office_does: State representatives write and vote on state laws and budgets, oversee state government, and represent their legislative district.
race_blurb: Incumbent Kevin Waters faces health-care professional Ben Christly and Army veteran and small-business owner Thomas Everett Haynes.

candidates.summary: Christly is a charter-review commissioner, Navy veteran, nurse, and former business owner who emphasizes schools, housing, health care, and bipartisan work.
highlights: Serves on the Clark County Charter Review Commission [S1]
Supports more housing, affordable health care, schools, and skills training [S1]

housing.basis: He calls for more housing, giving a supply direction without a specific policy mechanism.

experience.basis: His charter-review public service and extensive professional record balance new legislative perspective with institutional experience.

candidates.summary: Waters is the incumbent representative and former port commissioner and economic-development leader focused on infrastructure, housing, schools, and cost relief.
highlights: Offers legislative and prior port-commission experience [S1][S2]
Emphasizes roads, bridges, broadband, affordable housing, and cost relief [S1][S2]

experience.basis: He combines incumbency with prior port governance and economic-development leadership.

candidates.summary: Haynes is an Army veteran and cybersecurity and small-business professional who emphasizes gun rights and refuses PAC money.
highlights: Says he will not take PAC money [S1]
Lists affordable health care, schools, and housing as priorities [S1]

reform.basis: His explicit refusal of PAC money directly matches the reform pole.

experience.basis: He brings military, technical, and business experience but no documented elected record.
